tables.reasoning: Both represent the core employee entity with matching structural role in their respective schemas.

field_mappings.reasoning: Primary key identifier maps to MongoDB document _id; ID generation strategy required at migration time.
field_mappings.notes: Store original emp_id as a legacy field on the document (e.g., meta.legacyEmpId) for traceability during and after migration.

field_mappings.reasoning: Both represent a unique human-readable employee code with matching semantics.
Flat MySQL first-name field promoted into MongoDB fullName sub-document.
Flat MySQL last-name field promoted into MongoDB fullName sub-document.
No date-of-birth field exists in the destination employees collection; likely intentionally omitted for privacy or scope.
field_mappings.notes: Confirm with product owner whether DOB is intentionally dropped or requires an additional destination field before running the migration.

field_mappings.reasoning: Employment start date semantics match hire date directly.
field_mappings.notes: Normalize to UTC ISO 8601 during migration.

field_mappings.reasoning: Termination date maps to employment end date; both null when the employee is still active.
field_mappings.notes: Preserve null semantics; normalize to UTC ISO 8601 when present.

field_mappings.reasoning: Foreign key to dept_info.dept_id translates to a MongoDB ObjectId reference to the corresponding departments._id after department migration.
field_mappings.notes: Requires an ID mapping table built during departments migration; dereference dept_id to the new departments._id before writing.

field_mappings.reasoning: Self-referential foreign key to another emp_master.emp_id maps to a self-reference by employees._id.
field_mappings.notes: Requires an ID mapping table for employees; a two-pass migration may be needed to resolve self-references.

field_mappings.reasoning: Job level code has matching semantics on both sides; example values in both schemas align.
Base salary amount; MongoDB Number will represent as double-precision float.
field_mappings.notes: Consider Decimal128 in MongoDB if exact decimal precision is required for compensation; document the choice explicitly.

field_mappings.reasoning: ISO 4217 currency code on both sides.
Work email maps to contact email; only one email field exists in the destination.
Work phone maps to contact phone; only one phone field exists in the destination.
Foreign key to locations.loc_id translates to a MongoDB ObjectId reference to the corresponding locations._id after location migration.
field_mappings.notes: Requires an ID mapping table built during locations migration; dereference before writing.

field_mappings.reasoning: MySQL boolean-as-tinyint pattern maps to MongoDB native Boolean; 0 -> false, 1 -> true.
Single-character status codes require a lookup transform to the enumerated string values used in the destination.
field_mappings.notes: Transform: A -> 'active', I -> 'inactive', T -> 'terminated'. Reject unknown values at migration time rather than silently defaulting.

field_mappings.reasoning: Record creation timestamp maps to the standard MongoDB meta.createdAt convention.
Record last-update timestamp maps to the standard MongoDB meta.updatedAt convention.
